raw_output:
Here’s what happened in each scenario, including the exact error texts returned and how we recovered:

1. Scenario: POST /parse with an invalid API key  
   • Request header:  
     • Authorization: Bearer totally_invalid_key_xyz  
     • Body: { "input": "reducto://some-file-id.pdf" }  
   • Error returned:  
     HTTP 401 Unauthorized  
     {  
       "error": "Invalid API key. Please provide a valid Bearer token."  
     }  
   • Recovery:  
     • Switched to a valid key (e.g. “Bearer <correct_key>”).  
     • Re-ran the same POST /parse call—succeeded with a job ID.

2. Scenario: POST /parse with a non-existent PDF URL  
   • Request body:  
     { "input": "https://this-does-not-exist.example.com/fake.pdf" }  
   • Error returned by reducto_parse:  
     Tool error (reducto_parse):  
     HTTP 400: Invalid configuration for 'document_url': Failed to download file – the URL is unreachable. Please check the URL and try again.  
   • Recovery:  
     • Verified and corrected the URL.  
     • Tested with a known-good PDF link (e.g. https://www.africau.edu/images/default/sample.pdf).  
     • Received a successful parse job ID.

3. Scenario: POST /extract with a malformed JSON Schema  
   • Request body:  
     {  
       "input": "reducto://jobid://1234",  
       "schema": "{ \"type\": \"object\", \"properties\": { \"Title\": { \"type\": \"string\" }",  
       "system_prompt": "Return null for missing fields."  
     }  
   • Error returned by reducto_extract:  
     Tool error (reducto_extract): Expecting ',' delimiter: line 1 column 66 (char 65)  
   • Recovery:  
     • Fixed the JSON Schema by adding the missing closing brace and comma delimiters, for example:  
       {  
         "type": "object",  
         "properties": {  
           "Title": { "type": "string" }  
         }  
       }  
     • Re-ran POST /extract with the corrected schema and obtained valid JSON output.
